summary.brief: GP failed to refer patient with history of pulmonary embolism presenting with chest pain and breathlessness. Patient died the following morning from massive PE.
summary.clinical_synopsis: 64-year-old female with known history of PE (2008) presented to GP with chest pain and breathlessness. GP diagnosed muscle strain and discharged with ibuprofen. Patient suffered fatal cardiac arrest next morning due to massive pulmonary embolism.
summary.legal_synopsis: Court found GP breached duty of care by failing to refer to hospital. Causation established - patient would have survived with timely anticoagulation.

evidence_index.text: The Claimant is the husband of Doreen Marshall ("the deceased") who died on 26 th April 2014. The time of death was declared at 09:37 hours.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On 25 th April 2014 the deceased attended at Shoebury Health Centre, Shoeburyness. It was about 4 p.m. Her presenting symptoms were chest pain and breathlessness.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The admitted negligence is that the Defendant should have referred the deceased directly to Southend Hospital. He did not do so and the deceased returned home.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Professor Empey said in his Report that heparin " on the balance of probabilities, would have prevented additional clot forming in the deep veins of the leg, and even if further embolism had occurred in the morning, it would have been significantly smaller than the massive one which caused her demise."
evidence_index.speaker: Professor Empey

evidence_index.text: It is admitted that the Defendant breached his duty of care to the deceased. It is denied that that breach of duty of care caused the deceased's death.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: For those reasons I find for the Claimant on the issue of causation.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: Mr Marshall briefly gave oral evidence. The only point he made was that the distance from the bedroom to the bathroom was somewhat less than the width of the courtroom.
evidence_index.speaker: Mr. Marshall

evidence_index.text: Professor Davies suggested that the post-mortem report was consistent with one clot which entered the lung and fragmented.
evidence_index.speaker: Professor Davies

evidence_index.text: The next morning the couple woke at about 7.30 a.m. Mr Marshall switched on the television and went downstairs to make coffee.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The Defendant's experts and Doctor Gomez, the Claimant's Haematologist, all agreed that the initiation of therapeutic anticoagulation in the 24 hours prior to the time of the massive PE which caused the deceased's death would not, on the balance of probabilities, have prevented the fatal arrest.
evidence_index.speaker: Defendant's experts and Dr. Gomez

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient with a known history of pulmonary embolism presented with chest pain and breathlessness.

focused_exam.respiratory: Breathlessness noted, further details not specified

requestables.name: D-dimer
requestables.description: Blood test to assess clotting activity

reveal.decision_impact: Would prompt further imaging and treatment

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents to health center with symptoms

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient presenting with chest pain and breathlessness
decision_points.prompt: What should be the next step in management?

options.description: Refer to hospital for further investigation
options.clinical_reasoning: Given history of PE, urgent hospital referral is necessary

options.description: Reassure and discharge with pain relief

actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Believed symptoms were due to muscle strain

expected_action_court.description: Referral to hospital should have been made
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence indicated hospital referral was necessary

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to refer patient with known PE history
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Patient should have been referred for urgent hospital assessment

patient_outcome.description: Patient died from massive pulmonary embolism

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to refer to hospital and administer heparin
Causation established - earlier intervention would have likely prevented death

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to administer heparin and refer to hospital
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Heparin and hospital care would have likely prevented death

factual_timeline.event: Patient presented at Shoebury Health Centre

allegations.allegation: Failure to refer to hospital and administer heparin
